```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B2",
    "grammar_score": 0.8,
    "corrections": [
        "decition -> decision",
        "each my day -> each of my days / each day",
        "tips how to get it -> tips on how to get it",
        "options how to organize -> options on how to organize / options for organizing",
        "But very important thing -> But a very important thing"
    ],
    "feedback": "This is a strong B2 text. You have effectively used a variety of linking words and discourse markers (e.g., 'However,' 'Firstly,' 'For example,' 'Also, though') to structure your argument logically. The vocabulary is sophisticated for this level, with good use of phrases like 'overload yourself,' 'use all your resources,' and 'abuse this help.' While there are minor grammatical errors, such as article usage ('a very important thing') and prepositions ('tips how to get'), they do not impede communication. The sentence structure is varied and complex, particularly in the use of concession ('Also, though you shouldn't...')."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.45,
    "corrections": [
        "I want to say you abot -> I want to tell you about",
        "two my own tips -> two of my own tips",
        "what help me -> which help me",
        "more communicate -> communicate more",
        "look to life simplie -> look at life simply",
        "In futcher -> In the future",
        "give you a lot opportunity -> will give you a lot of opportunities",
        "need count your time -> need to count your time",
        "hasn't free time -> don't have free time",
        "try note your day -> try noting your day",
        "bad habbits -> bad habits",
        "Delite them -> Delete them"
    ],
    "feedback": "Your text demonstrates B1 level proficiency because you successfully organized your ideas using linking words ('Firstly', 'Secondly') and attempted complex sentence structures, such as relative clauses ('what help me') and conditional ideas. You also used some good vocabulary relevant to the topic, such as 'networking', 'lifestyle', and 'time management'. However, your score is lowered by frequent spelling errors ('futcher', 'beleave', 'delite') and basic grammar mistakes, particularly with verb patterns ('more communicate') and word forms ('hasn't free time'). Focus on improving your spelling and reviewing basic verb structures to make your writing clearer."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.6,
    "corrections": [
        "Hellow -> Hello",
        "and me to -> and me too / and so do I",
        "In first semester -> In the first semester",
        "wery -> very",
        "ideas how can I fix it -> ideas on how I can fix it / ideas how to fix it",
        "I will give you them -> I will give them to you",
        "enouth -> enough",
        "do sport well -> exercise regularly / do sports",
        "bust -> boost"
    ],
    "feedback": "This is a strong B1 response. You have successfully communicated your ideas and provided clear, logical tips using a well-structured list format. Your vocabulary is a strength here; using terms like 'prioritize', 'task-management system', and 'productivity' demonstrates a lexicon that exceeds basic level requirements. However, your score is held back by frequent spelling errors ('wery', 'enouth', 'bust' instead of 'boost') and basic grammar slips ('me to', 'how can I fix it'). With improved proofreading to fix these elementary errors, this text would easily approach a B2 level."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.55,
    "corrections": [
        "It is easily. -> It is easy.",
        "Now I want to some tips for became diffiecult study easilier. -> Now I want to give some tips to make difficult study easier.",
        "what your friend write -> what your friend writes",
        "It is not obviously tip -> It is not an obvious tip",
        "it really work -> it really works",
        "your brain relax -> your brain relaxes",
        "you can solve spend less time -> you can spend less time",
        "It is obviously. -> It is obvious.",
        "eductation esalier -> education easier"
    ],
    "feedback": "Your text demonstrates a good attempt at organizing your ideas using a clear structure (introduction, numbered tips, conclusion). You use some good vocabulary items such as 'approximately', 'solution', and 'iteration', which shows an ambition to express complex ideas. However, there are frequent errors in basic grammar, particularly with subject-verb agreement ('it really work', 'brain relax') and confusing adjectives with adverbs ('easily' instead of 'easy', 'obviously' instead of 'obvious'). While these errors are noticeable, they do not prevent the reader from understanding your advice. To reach the next level, focus on improving your grammatical accuracy and proofreading for spelling."
}
```

You also use complex sentence structures effectively, such as 'It's much easier to understand what you can do next with the list like this'. }
